_instructions: Create this contact flow in the Amazon Connect console:
1. Go to Amazon Connect instance → Contact Flows → Create contact flow
3. Build the flow using the visual editor with the blocks below
5. Note the Contact Flow ID from the flow's ARN (last segment)
IMPORTANT: Before creating this flow, you must:
- Create the VeratraceSandboxBot in Amazon Lex V2 (see lex-bot-definition.json)
- Add the bot as a resource in Connect: Instance Settings → Contact Flows → Amazon Lex → Add bot
- Create at least one queue: 'GeneralSupport' (Contact Flows → Queues → Add queue)
description: Routes contacts through Lex bot. Bot-resolved contacts disconnect. Failed/dispute contacts transfer to queue.

blocks.description: Invoke Lex bot to understand customer intent
blocks.textToSpeechPrompt: Welcome to Veratrace support. How can I help you today?

onSuccess._comment: Lex recognized an intent and fulfilled it

onError._comment: Lex failed to understand or FallbackIntent triggered

blocks.description: Check which intent was matched — route billing disputes to human
Bot resolved — set attributes and hang up
blocks.playPrompt: Your request has been handled. Thank you for contacting us!

blocks.description: Bot couldn't resolve — set attributes and transfer to queue
blocks.transferPrompt: Let me connect you with a specialist. Please hold.

_visualEditorSteps: 4.   If BillingDispute or FallbackIntent → Set Attributes (botResolved=false) → Set Queue (GeneralSupport) → Transfer to Queue
5.   Otherwise (ResetPassword, CheckBalance) → Set Attributes (botResolved=true) → Play Prompt → Disconnect
6. Error → Set Attributes (botResolved=false) → Set Queue (GeneralSupport) → Transfer to Queue
